claim: No Stable, release, conformance, numbering-accuracy, adapter-compatibility, package-parity, or recorded manual assistive-technology claim is made by this source contract.

semantics.nativeBasis: native input type=tel, optional labelled extension input, and hidden canonical form input
semantics.naming: A persistent visible Field label, native label, aria-label, or aria-labelledby names the phone input. Country and calling code are explicit localized descriptive text; the extension has its own visible label.
semantics.roleOverride: Consumer role overrides are outside the supported contract.

interaction.keyboard: The browser owns text editing, selection, undo, clipboard, composition, and validation. The component intercepts no browser, screen-reader, editor, or platform key.
interaction.pointer: Native input selection and browser pointer cancellation remain authoritative; formatting occurs only through the trusted adapter after input.
interaction.touch: The tel input and optional extension input preserve 44 CSS pixel targets and mobile-appropriate input modes.
interaction.speech: The visible Field label, country text, and extension label remain available for voice targeting without flag-only naming.
interaction.switchAccess: The phone and optional extension are real controls in DOM and scan order with no proxy stops.

formBehavior: The visible phone input owns native required and custom adapter validity but intentionally has no form name. A hidden same-form control serializes valid canonical E.164 output under name; extensionName serializes extension text separately. Disabled controls are omitted. Uncontrolled reset restores both defaults; controlled values remain consumer-owned.

states.synchronization: Visible text, adapter status, canonical E.164 value, extension, custom validity, Field relationships, and controlled or uncontrolled ownership update together; composition exposes no premature canonical value.

preferences.enhancedContrast: Validation includes programmatic state and text rather than color alone.
preferences.forcedColors: Uses system colors, visible double invalid borders, and explicit focus indicators.
preferences.reducedMotion: No value, validation, caret, or layout behavior depends on animation.

responsive.overflowPolicy: Country text, phone input, and extension wrap through intrinsic flex and a container adaptation without hiding labels, values, focus, or form capability.

internationalization.imePolicy: Composition text remains untouched and unvalidated until compositionend; no key or paste event is canceled.
internationalization.serialization: A valid adapter result serializes plus-prefixed E.164 digits only. Extension is a separate decimal string and is never appended to E.164. Display formatting is never submitted under the canonical name.

consumerResponsibilities: Provide a maintained, deterministic, trusted phone adapter with exact supported-country parsing, formatting, validation, and caret mapping.
Provide a localized country name with a visible calling code; do not substitute a flag-only control or infer country solely from a symbol.
Validate canonical phone and extension values again on the server and define extension policy for the receiving telephony system.
Use accurate autocomplete and application-level error/help text for the field purpose.
Do not load adapter code or numbering rules from untrusted registry metadata, URL state, or user input.
limitations: Mergora intentionally bundles no global numbering metadata and makes no validity claim beyond the supplied trusted adapter result and generic E.164 shape check.
The built-in extension control uses a separate decimal string; systems requiring pauses, waits, or service codes need a separately reviewed composed control.

Source-present-unreleased records do not include immutable generated artifacts, production adapter evidence, packed-consumer evidence, package/source parity, or current manual assistive-technology sessions.
